sentenceFrame: The cat is on the mat.
celebration: FIRST DECODABLE BOOK — the whole class reads

songs.title: C-C-Cat!
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
C! C! C-c-c!
(c… c… c…)

[Hook]
C! C! C-c-c!
What begins with C?
C-c-cat! C-c-cat!
It's a cat! (a cat!)

[Verse 2]
C! C! C-c-c!
What begins with C?
C-c-cup! C-c-cup!
It's a cup! (a cup!)

[Verse 3]
C! C! C-c-c!
What begins with C?
C-c-car! C-c-car!
It's a car! (a car!)

[Verse 4]
C! C! C-c-c!
What begins with C?
C-c-cow! C-c-cow!
It's a cow! (a cow!)

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! Not C!
(c… c… c…)

[Outro]
C-c-cat! C-c-cat!
It's a cat! (a cat!)
C! C! C-c-c!
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 65-72 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: The C sound is a crisp unvoiced /k/ click — NEVER 'see' (letter name) and NEVER 'kuh' (schwa). The '(c… c… c…)' lines are dry percussive /k/ clicks riding the hi-hats. Answer line 'It's a ___!' reuses the Week 1 frame.

songs.title: The Cat Is on the Mat
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(We can read. We can read!)

[Hook]
The cat is on the mat!
The cat is on the mat!
Where is the cat? (where? where?)
The cat is on the mat!

[Verse 2]
The mat is on the cat?!
The mat is on the cat?!
Where is the cat? (where? where?)
The mat is on the cat!

[Verse 3]
The cat is… on the mat?
(No! Look! Up! Up!)
Where is the cat? (where? where?)
The cat is UP! (Come down, cat!)

[Outro]
The cat is on the mat.
(shhh… the cat is asleep)
A potato is on the mat!
(PO-TA-TO!)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 65-72 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: This is THE anthem — the first sentence the class can read by themselves. One frame: 'The cat is on the mat.' Verse 2 flips it, verse 3 is the levitating-cat gag from the book. Whisper the outro, then the potato hits loud.

book.title: The Cat Is on the Mat

spreads.artNote: The familiar mat from Week 3, alone under the spotlight. The children already know this stage.
The Cat's grand entrance — a real cat regarding the mat with intense interest. Cast member #1 joins.

spreads.text: The cat is on the mat.
spreads.artNote: The cat sits perfectly, proudly centered on the mat. Triumphant.

spreads.text: The cat is on the mat.
spreads.artNote: Same sentence, new pose: the cat upside-down / sprawled ridiculously. Repetition with visual comedy.

spreads.text: The cat is… on the mat?
spreads.artNote: THE LEVITATING CAT. The cat floats serenely a foot above the mat. Text stays in frame; the picture carries the joke.

spreads.text: The cat is on the mat!
spreads.artNote: The cat lands back on the mat, fur slightly ruffled, dignity intact.

spreads.text: The mat is on the cat!
spreads.artNote: The flip: mat draped over the cat like a blanket, tail sticking out. The children READ the flip themselves — first decoding punchline in history.

spreads.text: The cat is on the mat.
spreads.artNote: Quiet spread. The cat curled asleep on the mat. Whisper-read this page.
The potato has appeared on the mat beside the sleeping cat. No noun printed — the class SHOUTS the word from the picture. One cat eye open.

sentences: The cat is on the mat.
The mat is on the cat.
A cat is on the mat.
The cat is at the mat.
extras: Heart-word cards: the, is (taught as sight words; note the th sound orally — never blended)
Moveable alphabet: build c-a-t, m-a-t, a-t, a-m
First-book certificate: every child reads the book aloud to Segina

teacherFocus: THE WEEK. Teach 'the' and 'is' as heart words (say the th sound orally, never blend it). Blend c-a-t. Then hand them the book and step back — every child reads 'The cat is on the mat.' aloud. Celebrate loudly. This is the moment the year is built around.
